data.content_ref: inline:The account's mechanism depends on the rapid diffusion of iron weaponry enabling peripheral groups to overwhelm bronze-age chariot aristocracies. However, the scope excludes Egypt, which also relied on a chariot-based military aristocracy and was a primary target of the Sea Peoples. The account fails to explain why Egypt's bronze-age chariot forces successfully repelled these same iron-armed, swarm-tactics infantry groups while all other palace civilizations were annihilated. This selective survival contradicts the claimed military-technological determinism.
